comment: CANONICAL debugger-side trace bring-up catalog (bkpt-packages/trace-support). Consumers bundle a snapshot at build/dev time (the ViewAlyzer app never fetches at runtime; a future app fetch would come from this repo). Offline user override: <userAppData>/ViewAlyzer/trace-support.json (same shape). Rows are keyed by debug ARCHITECTURE, not by chip: one row covers every part sharing a vendor debug-domain map, so this file stays a handful of rows forever. A row matches when probing its idcode_addr over the debug connection reads a nonzero device id (optionally restricted to dev_id). Its init ops then run through a read-modify-write interpreter: {addr, set} = write literal, {addr, or} = OR bits in, {addr, insert, mask} = (old & ~mask) | (insert & mask). The token $prescaler resolves to (cpu-clock-hz / swo-freq-hz) - 1. tpiu:false means the row replaces the standard ARM TPIU programming (parts with their own SWO block).

init.comment: DBGMCU_CR: D1/D3 debug clocks + trace clock enable
SWO SPPR: async NRZ (replaces the standard TPIU block)
RCC AHB4ENR: GPIOB clock (SWO pin = PB3)
GPIOB OSPEEDR: PB3 very high speed
DBGMCU_CR: TRACE_IOEN + DBG_SLEEP/STOP/STANDBY (keeps trace clocked through WFI idles)
